items.headline: State grant awarded to Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts
items.summary: The California Natural Resources Agency granted funding to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. The grant will expand cultural programming and arts education offerings at this South Bay landmark.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised evaluation criteria for biking and pedestrian projects. This plan will shape where the city invests in sidewalks, bike lanes, and pedestrian safety improvements over the coming years.

items.headline: Council changes vacancy rules, reduces something unspecified
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Council Vacancy Ordinance and reduce an unnamed item, but the agenda provides insufficient detail about what is actually changing or why it matters to residents.

items.headline: Major Elevator Upgrade Project Approved Downtown
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project at 1290 Ridder Park Dr represents significant infrastructure investment in San José's commercial district. This type of major building system upgrade typically indicates ongoing downtown revitalization and improved accessibility for workers and businesses.

items.headline: Lawsuit over Mary Avenue safety moves forward in Santa Clara court
items.summary: The Garden Gate Coalition is suing the City of Cupertino over Mary Avenue safety concerns. The council met with legal counsel on April 1st to discuss this ongoing litigation and anticipated related cases.

items.headline: Commercial/Industrial Development Planned on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial project at 1300 W San Carlos St will add new business space to a key commercial corridor. This development supports San José's continued economic growth and job creation in an established commercial area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty and retail incentive programs
items.summary: The city announced multiple economic development initiatives including a business license amnesty program, outreach to shopping centers, and incentives for retail and small businesses. These programs aim to support local commerce and formalize unlicensed businesses in the community.

items.headline: City seeking community input on federal housing grants
items.summary: Sunnyvale is reviewing applications for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding, which supports affordable housing and community development projects. This presentation shows how federal dollars are being allocated to local housing initiatives.
